channel.title: Alertmanager on sed-i sandbox
channel.description: Recent content in Alertmanager on sed-i sandbox

item.description: Setup alertmanager # Install and run alertmanager # > $ sudo snap install prometheus-alertmanager After which you can see that alertmanager is running with default parameters: > $ pgrep -a alertmanager /snap/prometheus-alertmanager/11/bin/alertmanager --config.file=/var/snap/prometheus-alertmanager/11/alertmanager.yml --storage.path=/var/snap/prometheus-alertmanager/common alertmanager configuration # > $ cat /var/snap/prometheus-alertmanager/11/alertmanager.yml

Alertmanager configuration # Anatomy of an alert # The two most important attributes of an alert are status and labels. In yaml form it looks like this: status: firing labels: alertname: "name of alert" service: "name of service" severety: "warning" Alerts like the one above can be posted to the alertmanager server via HTTP API, obtained via HTTP API or viewed on a web gui via a browser.
